title: centaurea elatior
intro: Centaurea elatior, also known as greater knapweed, is a flowering plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce pain and swelling, and prevent infection. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. Centaurea elatior can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
